Question: Do I need a visa to travel to India?
Answer:
Most foreign nationals require a visa to enter India. Visa requirements depend, among other things, on your nationality, travel history, personal circumstances and travel plans (including, purpose of travel). You are responsible for ensuring that you meet all applicable entry requirements for India, including obtaining any required visas or other travel authorizations. We recommend checking your visa eligibility and applying in advance through India's official visa channels. [**Government of India Visa Website Link**](https://indianvisaonline.gov.in/)
**Which Visa do I need?** The appropriate visa will depend on your individual circumstances, including your nationality, travel history, travel plans, and the purpose of your visit. We cannot determine which visa is appropriate for you or provide immigration advice. Before applying, please review the official requirements and, where necessary, seek guidance from the relevant Indian Embassy, Consulate, Indian Mission, or other official Indian visa authority in your country of residence.

Question: I need a Ticket Confirmation letter. How can I get one?
Answer:
If you require a Ticket Confirmation Letter to assist with your visa application for India, you can request one below. Please read the following before submitting:

- **You must have a confirmed ticket** to Devcon 8 India before requesting a letter
- Requests should be submitted **at least 90 days before** the event. Requests submitted fewer than two weeks before the event cannot be guaranteed, though we will make every effort to process them
- Please ensure all details are accurate as incomplete information may cause delays
- Provision of a ticket confirmation letter **doesn't guarantee visa approval**, which is made at the sole discretion of the Indian immigration authorities

[**Start your ticket confirmation letter request**](https://devcon.org/en/form/visa-collection-attendees/)

For questions, contact [**visa@devcon.org**](mailto:visa@devcon.org)

Question: What are the policies towards Refunds & Transfers?
Answer: Refunds will be permitted until the start of Devcon. General Admission tickets are anonymous and therefore transferrable. Discounted tickets are non-transferrable.

Question: Can I buy tickets on behalf of someone else?
Answer: General Admission tickets can be bought for someone else, but all discounts must be applied for & claimed by the individual who is attending.

Question: Is there a ticket purchase limit?
Answer: Yes — there is a limit of 2 tickets per order.

Question: How do I apply for a Sanctuary Tech Builder discount?
Answer: Sanctuary Tech Builder discount applications will open July 14.

Question: Can I transfer my ticket to someone else?
Answer: General Admission tickets are anonymous and transferrable. Name/email can be changed using the link in your original Order Confirmation email. **Discounted tickets are non-transferrable**.

Question: If I buy a ticket, and then I am accepted to Speak, can I get a refund for the original ticket I purchased?
Answer: Yes, if you are accepted as a speaker, you can request a refund for your original ticket purchase.

Question: What if I only need to cancel some tickets on an order with multiple?
Answer: You can request a partial cancellation by contacting our support team at [support@devcon.org](mailto:support@devcon.org).

Question: Are there discounts for Indian residents?
Answer: Yes — we have limited discounted tickets reserved for Indian residents. You can verify your residency using on [our ticket shop](https://devcon.org/en/tickets/store/) with Self.

Question: Are there group or team discounts available?
Answer: While we do not offer group or team tickets, we offer plenty of discounts to individual applicants. You may also consider becoming a Supporter in our [Supporter Program](https://devcon.org/en/supporters/).

Question: Will there be opportunities to obtain discounted tickets?
Answer:
Yes! There will be multiple ways to obtain discounted tickets this year:

- **Local Discounts** — We will have limited tickets reserved for Indian residents.
- **Community Discounts** — This will consist of groups like Protocol Guild members, OSS Contributors and more and can be independently claimed.
- **Applications** — This will include Sanctuary Tech Builder Discounts, Student Discounts, and Youth Tickets for those ages 3-17.

Question: I'm a student, can I attend Devcon?
Answer:
Yes — we offer discounted tickets for Indian and International students to join us at Devcon 8 India. Students should fill out our [application form](https://devcon.org/en/form/student-application/) and if approved, we’ll send you a voucher link to purchase your ticket. Indian and International Student tickets are priced at $25 and $99 respectively.

**There will be 2 rounds of application review:**

- **Round 1 –** Apply by **June 12**, responses sent by July 15.
- **Round 2** – Apply by **August 14**, responses sent by Sept. 7.

**All applicants should meet the following criteria:**

- Currently enrolled in an accredited university degree program (Bachelor, Master, or PhD)
- Studying fields such as computer science, engineering, mathematics, economics, law, governance, public policy, or other relevant disciplines
- Students contributing to research, open-source projects, or academic work related to blockchain, cryptography, governance, or digital public infrastructure
- Students involved in university research groups, blockchain clubs, policy initiatives, or developer communities
- Students with a demonstrated interest in Ethereum and open technologies

Please note: Short-term courses, bootcamps, and online-only programs are not eligible.

Question: I'm from an academic background, how can I / my community get involved in Devcon?
Answer:
Please check out our [Academic Program](https://devcon.org/en/academic-program/) to see if you and your community may be eligible.

Alternatively, students from India and abroad are welcome to [apply for a discounted Student ticket](https://devcon.org/en/form/student-application/). If you have any questions, feel free to reach out to us at [support@devcon.org](mailto:support@devcon.org).

Question: If I am accepted for a discount after buying a full-priced ticket, can I get a refund of the difference?
Answer:
Yes — You can:

1. **Cancel** your original order using the link in your confirmation email, then **redeem** the new Voucher Code, OR
2. Reach out to support@devcon.org.

Question: What airport(s) should I fly into?
Answer: The closest airport to Mumbai is the Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj International Airport (BOM). It’s approximately 10–12km from BKC (about 30–45 minutes by taxi).

Question: Which fiat payment methods are accepted?
Answer:
Payment with ETH is strongly encouraged. 

All major debit and credit card providers are accepted and you can checkout using Google and/or Apple Pay.

**Please note:** Payments made via Fiat will be processed on our **Pretix Store**.

Question: I'm having trouble completing my payment – what should I do?
Answer: Follow any on-screen steps and ensure you have enough funds to complete the transaction. If this isn’t working, reach out to our Support team at [<u>support@devcon.org</u>](mailto:support@devcon.org) – we’ll be happy to help.

Question: Are there grants or scholarships available for those who can't afford a ticket?
Answer: We are currently offering a range of discounts to keep Devcon accessible, including Community, Builder, and Student tickets. We're also working with local universities and Ethereum communities to ensure we're getting tickets into the hands of the most deserving people on the ground.

Question: Is the event accessible for attendees with disabilities?
Answer: Yes, the venue is accessible to those with disabilities.

Question: When will General ticket sales start?
Answer:
General Admission ticket sales for Devcon India began with our ETH-only Early Bird sale in May.

Additional General Admission ticket waves will begin on July 14.

Question: What is the dress code / are there any venue rules I should be aware of?
Answer:
While there is no specific dress code for Devcon, we will uphold a "No shirt, no shoes, no entry" rule. 

No weapons, drugs, drones, or animals are permitted inside the venue. Smoking or chewing of tobacco is prohibited.

Question: When will I get my ticket?
Answer: You can add your ticket to your Apple or Google Wallet immediately after placing your order. We will send a PDF containing your ticket QR a few days prior to Devcon.

Question: Can I purchase tickets with crypto?
Answer:
Yes, and we strongly encourage it! 

You will be able to choose between Credit Card or Crypto to pay for your ticket.

**Please note**: Payment with Fiat will be marked up significantly for most products.

More details can be found on [our main ticket page](https://devcon.org/en/tickets/).

Question: How can I cancel my order?
Answer: You can cancel your order by returning to your Order page found in your original confirmation email & clicking '**Cancel Order**'.

Question: Will sessions be recorded or streamed for those who can't attend in person?
Answer: Yes, sessions will be recorded & streamed on our [YouTube channel](https://www.youtube.com/@EthereumFoundation) for those who cannot attend Devcon.

Question: Which cryptocurrencies are accepted for payment?
Answer: We will only be accepting **ETH on L1** for purchases with Crypto.

Question: What does my ticket include access to?
Answer: All 4-day tickets include full conference access, catering, and hot drinks.

Question: Can I purchase tickets outside of Waves?
Answer:
No — General Admission tickets are only available during waves. However, we will have limited free & discounted tickets available on [our ticket shop](https://devcon.org/tickets/store) throughout the year.

We will also be featuring a **Patron Ticket** this year; this ticket can be bought outside of waves to directly support the Scholarship Program.

Question: Who can apply for a discounted ticket?
Answer: [Discounted tickets](https://devcon.org/en/tickets/) are available through applications for Students, Sanctuary Tech Builders, and ecosystem contributors. Each category has its own eligibility criteria and pricing. Community Discounts can be independently claimed. Application-based discounts are reviewed on a rolling basis and tickets are limited.

Question: Tickets are sold out - how can I attend?
Answer: [**Subscribe to our newletter**](https://paragraph.com/@efevents?modal=subscribe) for updates and reminders, and [**follow us on X**](https://x.com/EFDevcon) to stay up to date on future ticket releases.

Question: I’m new to Ethereum and decentralized technologies — can I still participate?
Answer:
Absolutely! Our events are perfect for curious beginners looking to explore the technologies that keep the internet free and self-sovereign.

If you value **censorship resistance, OSS, privacy** or **security** – you’ll feel at home at Devcon.

Question: I plan on bringing my child to Devcon with me. Do they need a ticket?
Answer:
If your child is between the ages of 3–17, they will need a Youth ticket. A Youth Ticket is only valid for those under 18.

Whether you're a parent/guardian buying on behalf of children, or you're under-18 and want to attend Devcon with parental consent, fill out [**our Youth Ticket request form**](https://devcon.org/en/form/youth-ticket/) to apply.

**How to apply**

- Complete the application form with your details, parental consent, and the number of tickets required
- We'll review your application and email you with a decision
- If approved, you'll receive a voucher link to complete your purchase.

**Youth tickets are limited — we recommend applying early.**

Question: Are there recommended hotels or accommodation near the venue?
Answer:
Check out our [**'Your Friend's Guide to Mumbai' X article**](https://x.com/EFDevcon/status/2049558668165754933?s=20) to get you started!

The team at Tripsha have been working with us to set up a handy page to [**find accommodation in Mumbai**](https://app.tripsha.com/event/69b89b4703d64d0002894a82) for Devcon. 

The hotels have designated rooms at **discounted rates** and special perks specifically for Devcon attendees. Rooms will be allocated entirely on a **first-come-first-served basis** and are subject to availability.

Question: Do I need to bring ID to registration to collect my wristband/badge?
Answer: All Discounted ticket types (Locals, Builder, Student, Youth) **must show ID** at Registration. All other ticket types can enter with just their QR code.

Question: If I buy a ticket, but I am unable to obtain an appropriate visa or other entry permission required to attend Devcon, can I get a refund?
Answer:
Yes. In the unfortunate event your Visa application is rejected, we will refund the cost of your ticket back to the original payment method.

You must **Cancel your Order** on your original Order Confirmation Page (_sent via email_) to begin the process, or reach out to us at support@devcon.org if your order contains more than 1 ticket.

Orders paid for with Fiat will be automatically refunded.

Orders paid for with Crypto will need to be manually refunded by our team. **Please allow us up to 2 weeks to process Crypto refunds.**

Question: If I buy a ticket, but my Visa is rejected, can I get a refund of my Devcon ticket?
Answer:
Yes. In the unfortunate event your Visa application is rejected, we will refund the cost of your ticket back to the original payment method.

You must **Cancel your Order** on your original Order Confirmation Page (_sent via email_) to begin the process, or reach out to us at support@devcon.org if your order contains more than 1 ticket.

Orders paid for with Fiat will be automatically refunded.

Orders paid for with Crypto will need to be manually refunded by our team. **Please allow us up to 2 weeks to process Crypto refunds.**

Question: I am under 18 years old. Can I attend?
Answer:
Yes — Parents or Youth can [apply for a Youth Ticket](https://devcon.org/en/form/youth-ticket/). 

**Note**: Anyone under 16 years old must be accompanied by a parent/guardian.
